title: Perseus Visits Again
tagline: He said he'd come back. She said he could. That was three months ago. She has been waiting. She would never say that.
arc_note: MEDUSA ARC — Second meeting. Significant shift: Medusa is not hostile. She has been thinking about the first visit. The stone owl she showed Perseus in EP003 reappears. This episode ends with something new: she asks Perseus to come back again. First time she's asked for something.

characters.focus_duo: Perseus and Mini Medusa — second meeting, warmer, still awkward, genuine
characters.medusa_state: She has added to her stone garden since EP003 — new sculptures, better quality. She's been practicing. She won't say why.

scenes.title: Perseus Comes Back
scenes.narration: Perseus had thought about going back to Medusa's garden for three months. He had almost gone four times. He had turned around three of those times. The fourth time he made it to the valley entrance. He stood there. He checked his shield was polished. It was. He went in. He called: 'It's Perseus.' There was silence. Then: 'I know. I can hear your sandals.' He had not realized his sandals made noise.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Perseus at the entrance of Medusa's stone garden valley, shield polished and held ready (as mirror), slightly nervous. Calling in. Medusa's voice from off-panel. Her stone garden visible — more sculptures than before, more intricate. He can't see her yet. He proceeds carefully. 16:9.

scenes.title: The New Garden
scenes.narration: The garden had new sculptures. Better ones — more detailed, more carefully positioned. Perseus counted eighteen new pieces since his last visit. He noticed they were arranged differently — not random, compositional. He said: 'You've been busy.' She said: 'I always work.' He said: 'These are better than the ones from before.' There was a pause. She said: 'Yes. I know.' She was not good at receiving compliments but she had heard it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Perseus using his shield to navigate the garden, seeing the new sculptures — more detailed animals, a stone figure that looks like it's dancing, careful arrangement. Mini Medusa across the garden, golden sunglasses, watching him look at her work. The snake-hair quiet, listening. She's heard the compliment. She won't say much about it. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Stone Owl
scenes.narration: Perseus found the stone owl she'd shown him last time — his favorite from the first visit. It was in a better spot now, elevated on a small stone pedestal. He said: 'You moved it.' She said: 'It deserved better placement.' He said: 'It still looks like Archie.' The snakes rustled. Medusa said: 'That was not intentional.' One of the snakes — Dreamy, the most honest one — swayed in a motion that clearly meant: it was a little intentional.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Perseus looking at the stone owl (clearly Archie-shaped) now on a better pedestal. Mini Medusa nearby, sunglasses on, very casual. The snake named Dreamy sways in a 'a little intentional' motion. Perseus trying not to smile. Medusa looking away. 16:9.

scenes.title: What She Asks
scenes.narration: At the end of the visit, as Perseus was heading back to the valley entrance, Medusa said something quickly and turned away: 'There is a garden in the eastern valley I cannot visit. I have heard the flowers there are extraordinary. You could describe it.' She said it very fast and was already looking at her sculptures before he finished processing it. Perseus understood: she wanted to know what she couldn't see safely. He said: 'I'll go look. I'll tell you everything.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. QUIET MOMENT — Medusa saying it quickly, turned away. Perseus hearing it. The understanding that passes: she can't go somewhere because of what she is. He offers simply and without making it a big thing. Her posture shifts slightly — she wasn't sure he'd say yes. He does. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Eastern Valley
scenes.narration: Perseus visited the eastern valley that afternoon. He described everything: the exact colors of the flowers (seventeen shades of purple), the way the light came through the cliff gap, the smell, a family of stone-colored rabbits that blended into the grey rocks. He wrote it all down. He brought the notes back to the valley entrance and read them out. Medusa listened without moving. Her snakes were completely quiet.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Split — Perseus in the beautiful eastern valley genuinely observing, writing notes. Then Perseus at the valley entrance reading aloud. Medusa sitting just inside, facing away, listening. All seven snakes completely still. She is very present for this. It is a gift he can give her that nobody else would think to. 16:9.

scenes.title: Come Back
scenes.narration: When Perseus finished reading, Medusa said: 'Thank you.' Two words she had apparently not said before to anyone except her snakes. Then she said: 'You can come back.' Perseus said: 'I know. You said last time.' She said: 'I'm saying it again.' He left. He was smiling the whole way home. He didn't tell anyone why. It felt like it belonged to both of them.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Perseus leaving the valley, smiling. Behind him, Medusa at the valley edge — just briefly visible before he rounds the corner. Her expression is obscured by the sunglasses but her posture is lighter. He smiles the whole walk home. He doesn't tell anyone. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: Sometimes the most powerful thing you can give someone is access to something they can't reach themselves — a description, a witness, a set of eyes that go where they can't. Perseus became Medusa's eyes in the world. Medusa became someone who asked for what she needed. Both of those things are hard. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — Perseus walking home at golden hour, notes in hand. Small visual in corner: the stone owl on its pedestal in the garden. The eastern valley flowers in another corner. Warm and quiet. 16:9.

lesson: The most generous thing you can give someone is access — to a place, a sight, an experience they can't reach on their own.
medusa_arc_note: EP012: Second meeting. Medusa asks for something (first time). Perseus becomes her eyes in the world she can't safely visit. Relationship deepens. Next Medusa appearance: EP014 — reluctant ally.
youtube_title: Perseus Visits Mini Medusa AGAIN! 🐍💜 | Little Olympus Episode 12
next_episode_preview: Episode 13 — Achilles's Best Race! The biggest race of the year. And something feels different about his heel today.
